question_text: A tuning fork with a frequency of 256 Hz vibrates in air. How many complete oscillations does the tuning fork complete in 0.5 seconds?
explanation: Frequency tells us how many complete oscillations happen in one second. A 256 Hz tuning fork completes 256 oscillations per second. To find oscillations in 0.5 seconds, multiply: 256 × 0.5 = 128 oscillations.
